Welcome to the official website of the 2026 4th International Conference on Computers and Automation (CompAuto 2026). The conference will take place in Perth, Australia from September 10 to 12, 2026. CompAuto 2026 will bring together researchers from related disciplines to discuss emerging challenges, exchange innovative ideas, and share the latest results and technical advancements in the fields of computers and automation. This conference provides a platform for delegates from diverse research areas to engage in face-to-face discussions, exchange new ideas and practical experiences, and establish collaborative business or research relationships. It also serves as an opportunity to identify global partners for future cooperation in Computers and Automation.
We encourage submissions that present new methodologies, advanced prototypes, systems, tools, and techniques, as well as survey papers highlighting future research directions. Authors are invited to submit original work in any of the conferenceâs focus areas. Accepted papers must be presented at the conference by one of the authors and will be published in the Proceedings of CompAuto 2026 (with an ISBN). Submissions will be evaluated based on quality, relevance, and originality. Both comprehensive research reports and work-in-progress reports are welcomed.
Authors are invited to submitÂ significant, original, complete, and previously unpublished paper. Original contributionsÂ are invited across, but not limited to the following tracks:
Track 1: Software Design and Analysis
Track 2: Parallel and Distributed Systems
Track 3: Database and Data Science
Track 4 : Multimedia, Computer Vision and Image Processing
Track 5: Intelligent Information Systems and Management
Track 6: AI, Agent Systems and Ambient Intelligence
Track 7: Signal Processing, Sensors, Communication Systems
Track 8: Intelligent Control Systems and Robotics Technology

Submission Methods

*Abstract Submission-
Accepted

		authors will be invited to give an oral/poster presentation on site
Prior to make the submission, please prepare your submission according to theÂ
Manuscript Formatting guidelines
. Full paper should be at least 4 full pages and at most 10 pages under the format. One regular registration only covers one paper within 6 pages. Additional pages will be charged.
Send the Full paper & Abstract to
Electronic 
		Submission System
, contact with us via
icca_conf@outlook.com
if you have any 
		questions.
Policy on Submissions
CompAuto will not accept any paper which, at the time of submission, is 
		  under review, is accepted for publication, or has already been published 
		  in another conference or a journal. Authors are also expected not to 
		  submit their papers elsewhere during the CompAuto reviewing period.
CompAuto is utterly intolerant of plagiarism. Submitted papers are expected 
		  to contain original work executed by the authors with adequate, proper 
		  and scholarly citations to the work of others. It is the job of the 
		  authors to clearly identify both their own contribution(s) and also 
		  published results / techniques on which they depend or build. Reviewers 
		  are charged to ensure these standards are met.
If any author is found to commit an act of plagiarism, the following 
		acts of sanction will be taken:
Reject the article submitted or delete the article from the 
			final publications.
Report the authors violation to his/her supervisor(s) and 
			affiliated institution(s)
Report the authors violation to the appropriate overseeing 
			office of academic ethics and research funding agency.
Reserve the right to publish the authors name(s), the title of 
			the article, the name(s) of the affiliated institution and the 
			details of misconduct, etc. of the plagiarist

Conference Venue
Murdoch University
Address: 90 South St, Murdoch WA 6150, Australia
Murdoch Maps
Located on South Street in Murdoch, the campus is close to Perth’s CBD and just 8km from Fremantle. Murdoch University encourages staff, students and visitors to consider sustainable options when travelling to and from our campuses. With links to bicycle networks and public transport, choosing an affordable, convenient or environmentally sustainable way to get here is easy.
Public Transport
The Perth campus is a short walk from the Murdoch Train Staton on the Perth-Mandurah line; the main north-south rail line that runs from Perth Underground Station to Mandurah Station. Refer to the Transperth Mandurah Line timetable.
Transperth offer parking facilities for bicycles and cars at most stations, allowing you to choose Park and Ride as a sustainable transport option.Â
Bus routes connect the campus to Murdoch Train Station, Fremantle, and surrounding suburbs. Buses are frequent and stops are located on South St (routes 503, 504, 513), Murdoch Drive (routes 510, 511), and Discovery Way (routes 998, 999, 204, 205, 206, 207).Â Â

Important information regarding invitation letters (please read):
The invitation letter will be issued after the successful registration.
Your name must be listed exactly as it appears on your passport. Any differences between the name on your passport and the name on your invitation letter or other documentation could lead to a delay and/or denial of your visa.
Please note CompAuto Conferences and the organizing committee of the conference are not authorized to assist with the VISA process beyond providing the Notification of Invitation Letter issued by CompAuto Conferences and conference Committee Board. Should your application be denied, CompAuto Conferences and the organizing committee of the conference cannot change the decision of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, nor will CompAuto Conference and the organizing committee of the conference engage in discussion or correspondence with the MOFA or the Embassy on behalf of the applicant. The registration fee cannot be refunded when the VISA application of individual being denied.
It takes time for the Embassy to process the VISA application, please register as early as you can to make sure you have enough time.
Guidline for VISA
All the latest information relating to obtaining an Australian visa, please visit:

Please explain why the topic of the special session is novel and timely, why it is relevant to the CompAuto community and how it possibly fits within the innovation themes listed above.

Â Provide a list of all talks, speakers, and co-authors (in each associated paper). Technical sessions are normally expected to have 5 papers. Welcome to the official website of the 2026 4th International Conference on Computers and Automation (CompAuto 2026). The conference will take place in Perth, Australia from September 10 to 12, 2026. CompAuto 2026 will bring together researchers from related disciplines to discuss emerging challenges, exchange innovative ideas, and share the latest results and technical advancements in the fields of computers and automation. This conference provides a platform for delegates from diverse research areas to engage in face-to-face discussions, exchange new ideas and practical experiences, and establish collaborative business or research relationships. It also serves as an opportunity to identify global partners for future cooperation in Computers and Automation.

Policy on Submissions
CompAuto will not accept any paper which, at the time of submission, is 
		  under review, is accepted for publication, or has already been published 
		  in another conference or a journal. Authors are also expected not to 
		  submit their papers elsewhere during the CompAuto reviewing period.
CompAuto is utterly intolerant of plagiarism. Submitted papers are expected 
		  to contain original work executed by the authors with adequate, proper 
		  and scholarly citations to the work of others. It is the job of the 
		  authors to clearly identify both their own contribution(s) and also 
		  published results / techniques on which they depend or build. Reviewers 
		  are charged to ensure these standards are met.
If any author is found to commit an act of plagiarism, the following 
		acts of sanction will be taken:
Reject the article submitted or delete the article from the 
			final publications.
Report the authors violation to his/her supervisor(s) and 
			affiliated institution(s)
Report the authors violation to the appropriate overseeing 
			office of academic ethics and research funding agency.
Reserve the right to publish the authors name(s), the title of 
			the article, the name(s) of the affiliated institution and the 
			details of misconduct, etc. of the plagiarist

It's an annual conference whose aim is to seek submissions from academia, industry, and government institutions presenting novel research on computers and automation

Welcome to the official website of the 2026 4th International Conference on Computers and Automation (CompAuto 2026). The conference will take place in Perth, Australia from September 10 to 12, 2026. CompAuto 2026 will bring together researchers from related disciplines to discuss emerging challenges, exchange innovative ideas, and share the latest results and technical advancements in the fields of computers and automation. This conference provides a platform for delegates from diverse research areas to engage in face-to-face discussions, exchange new ideas and practical experiences, and establish collaborative business or research relationships. It also serves as an opportunity to identify global partners for future cooperation in Computers and Automation.
We encourage submissions that present new methodologies, advanced prototypes, systems, tools, and techniques, as well as survey papers highlighting future research directions. Authors are invited to submit original work in any of the conferenceâs focus areas. Accepted papers must be presented at the conference by one of the authors and will be published in the Proceedings of CompAuto 2026 (with an ISBN). Submissions will be evaluated based on quality, relevance, and originality. Both comprehensive research reports and work-in-progress reports are welcomed.

Reject the article submitted or delete the article from the

Located on South Street in Murdoch, the campus is close to Perth’s CBD and just 8km from Fremantle. Murdoch University encourages staff, students and visitors to consider sustainable options when travelling to and from our campuses. With links to bicycle networks and public transport, choosing an affordable, convenient or environmentally sustainable way to get here is easy.

The Perth campus is a short walk from the Murdoch Train Staton on the Perth-Mandurah line; the main north-south rail line that runs from Perth Underground Station to Mandurah Station. Refer to the Transperth Mandurah Line timetable.

Welcome to the official website of the 2026 4th International Conference on Computers and Automation (CompAuto 2026). The conference will take place in Perth, Australia from September 10 to 12, 2026. CompAuto 2026 will bring together researchers from related disciplines to discuss emerging challenges, exchange innovative ideas, and share the latest results and technical advancements in the fields of computers and automation. This conference provides a platform for delegates from diverse research areas to engage in face-to-face discussions, exchange new ideas and practical experiences, and establish collaborative business or research relationships. It also serves as an opportunity to identify global partners for future cooperation in Computers and Automation.
We encourage submissions that present new methodologies, advanced prototypes, systems, tools, and techniques, as well as survey papers highlighting future research directions. Authors are invited to submit original work in any of the conferenceâs focus areas. Submissions will be evaluated based on quality, relevance, and originality. Both comprehensive research reports and work-in-progress reports are welcomed.

Policy on Submissions
CompAuto will not accept any paper which, at the time of submission, is 
		  under review, is accepted for publication, or has already been published 
		  in another conference or a journal. Authors are also expected not to 
		  submit their papers elsewhere during the CompAuto reviewing period.
CompAuto is utterly intolerant of plagiarism. Submitted papers are expected 
		  to contain original work executed by the authors with adequate, proper 
		  and scholarly citations to the work of others. It is the job of the 
		  authors to clearly identify both their own contribution(s) and also 
		  published results / techniques on which they depend or build. Reviewers 
		  are charged to ensure these standards are met.
If any author is found to commit an act of plagiarism, the following 
		acts of sanction will be taken:
Reject the article submitted or delete the article from the 
			final publications.
Report the authors violation to his/her supervisor(s) and 
			affiliated institution(s)
Report the authors violation to the appropriate overseeing 
			office of academic ethics and research funding agency.
Reserve the right to publish the authors name(s), the title of 
			the article, the name(s) of the affiliated institution and the 
			details of misconduct, etc. of the plagiarist

Murdoch University encourages staff, students and visitors to consider sustainable options when travelling to and from our campuses. With links to bicycle networks and public transport, choosing an affordable, convenient or environmentally sustainable way to get here is easy.
Public Transport
The Perth campus is a short walk from the Murdoch Train Staton on the Perth-Mandurah line; the main north-south rail line that runs from Perth Underground Station to Mandurah Station. Refer to the Transperth Mandurah Line timetable.
Transperth offer parking facilities for bicycles and cars at most stations, allowing you to choose Park and Ride as a sustainable transport option.Â
Bus routes connect the campus to Murdoch Train Station, Fremantle, and surrounding suburbs. Buses are frequent and stops are located on South St (routes 503, 504, 513), Murdoch Drive (routes 510, 511), and Discovery Way (routes 998, 999, 204, 205, 206, 207).Â Â

CompAuto 2026 will bring together researchers from related disciplines to discuss emerging challenges, exchange innovative ideas, and share the latest results and technical advancements in the fields of computers and automation. Policy on Submissions
CompAuto will not accept any paper which, at the time of submission, is 
		  under review, is accepted for publication, or has already been published 
		  in another conference or a journal. Authors are also expected not to 
		  submit their papers elsewhere during the CompAuto reviewing period.
CompAuto is utterly intolerant of plagiarism. Submitted papers are expected 
		  to contain original work executed by the authors with adequate, proper 
		  and scholarly citations to the work of others. It is the job of the 
		  authors to clearly identify both their own contribution(s) and also 
		  published results / techniques on which they depend or build. Reviewers 
		  are charged to ensure these standards are met.
If any author is found to commit an act of plagiarism, the following 
		acts of sanction will be taken:
Reject the article submitted or delete the article from the 
			final publications.
Report the authors violation to his/her supervisor(s) and 
			affiliated institution(s)
Report the authors violation to the appropriate overseeing 
			office of academic ethics and research funding agency.
Reserve the right to publish the authors name(s), the title of

- The duration of a presentation slot isÂ

. Personal laptops are not allowed for presentations.

- Presenters are invited to report to the session chair 15 minutes BEFORE the start of the session. Please wear formal clothes or national characteristics of clothing for participation.

Â
